revision.comment: Auto-generated page using script written by Immanuelle, either as a placeholder redirect or to make bots recognize the page as made by Immanuelle
revision.text: #redirect[[en:Pehui-djat]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = F22-G43-Z1*Z1 U28-M17-M17-t N14-N33:N33:N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = Pehui-djat
''{{unicode|Pḥ.wj ḏ3j.t}}'' }} '''Pehui-djat''' ist die Bezeichnung eines [[Altes Ägypten|altägyptischen]] [[Dekade (Altägypten)|Dekans]], der drei [[Baktiu|Dekan-Sterne]] umfasste. Da alle zugehörigen [[Himmelsobjekt]]e sehr lichtschwach sind, ist kein [[Stern|Hauptstern]] besonders auffällig. Die Dekansterne lassen sich auf die Region der [[Sternbild]]er [[Wasserschlange (Sternbild)|Wasserschlange]] und [[Segel des Schiffs (Sternbild)|Segel des Schiffs]] eingrenzen. In den Dekanlisten der [[Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|Sethos-Schrift]] repräsentierte Pehui-djat am Leib der [[Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|Nut]] den vierten Dekan. Der [[heliakisch]]e Aufgang war für den [[Schemu#Datierungen|6. Schemu II]] angesetzt und hatte als Datierungsgrundlage die verfügte Anordnung unter [[Sesostris III.]] ([[12. Dynastie]]) in dessen siebtem Regierungsjahr. == Siehe auch == * [[Liste der Dekan-Sterne im Nutbuch]] == Literatur == * [[Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, S. 95. * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (u. a.), Kopenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, S. 62–67 und S. 385. [[Kategorie:Dekanstern (Altägypten)]]

{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = F22-G43-Z1*Z1 U28-M17-M17-t N14-N33:N33:N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Pehui-djat
''Pḥ.wj-ḏ3j.t'' |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = Pehui-djat }} '''Pehui-djat''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)}}, which comprised three {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan stars). Since all associated {{ill|Celestial object|de|Himmelsobjekt}}s are very faint, no {{ill|Star|de|Stern}} stands out as particularly prominent. The decan stars can be localized within the regions of the constellations {{ill|Hydra (constellation)|de|Wasserschlange (Sternbild)}} and {{ill|Vela (constellation)|de|Segel des Schiffs}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Pehui-djat represented the fourth decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Shemu#Dating|lt=6th Schemu II|de|6. Schemu II}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 95. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. [[Category:Decan star (Ancient Egypt)]] [[ja:ペフイ・ジャット]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[de:Pehui-djat]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Untranslated pages]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = F22-G43-Z1*Z1 U28-M17-M17-t N14-N33:N33:N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Pehui-djat
''Pḥ.wj-ḏ3j.t'' |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = Pehui-djat }} '''Pehui-djat''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)}}, which comprised three {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan stars). Since all associated {{ill|Celestial object|de|Himmelsobjekt}}s are very faint, no {{ill|Star|de|Stern}} stands out as particularly prominent. The decan stars can be localized within the regions of the constellations {{ill|Hydra (constellation)|de|Wasserschlange (Sternbild)}} and {{ill|Vela (constellation)|de|Segel des Schiffs}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Pehui-djat represented the fourth decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Shemu#Dating|lt=6th Schemu II|de|6. Schemu II}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 95. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. [[Category:Decan star (Ancient Egypt)]] [[ja:ペフイ・ジャット]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]] [[Category:new pages]][[en:draft:Pehui-djat]] [[de:Pehui-djat]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Untranslated pages]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:new pages]]

{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = F22-G43-Z1*Z1 U28-M17-M17-t N14-N33:N33:N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Pehui-djat
''Pḥ.wj-ḏ3j.t'' |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = Pehui-djat }} '''Pehui-djat''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)}}, which comprised three {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan stars). Since all associated {{ill|Celestial object|de|Himmelsobjekt}}s are very faint, no {{ill|Star|de|Stern}} stands out as particularly prominent. The decan stars can be localized within the regions of the constellations {{ill|Hydra (constellation)|de|Wasserschlange (Sternbild)}} and {{ill|Vela (constellation)|de|Segel des Schiffs}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Pehui-djat represented the fourth decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Shemu#Dating|lt=6th Schemu II|de|6. Schemu II}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * {{illChristian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 95. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. {{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = F22-G43-Z1*Z1 U28-M17-M17-t N14-N33:N33:N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Pehui-djat
''Pḥ.wj-ḏ3j.t'' |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = Pehui-djat }} '''Pehui-djat''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan}}, which comprised three {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan stars). Since all associated {{ill|Celestial object|de|Himmelsobjekt}}s are very faint, no {{ill|Star|de|Stern}} stands out as particularly prominent. The decan stars can be localized within the regions of the constellations {{ill|Hydra (constellation)|de|Wasserschlange (Sternbild)}} and {{ill|Vela (constellation)|de|Segel des Schiffs}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Pehui-djat represented the fourth decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|lt=Shemu#Dating|lt=6th Schemu II|de|6. Schemu II}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * {{Ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. {{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = F22-G43-Z1*Z1 U28-M17-M17-t N14-N33:N33:N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Pehui-djat
''Pḥ.wj-ḏ3j.t'' |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = Pehui-djat }} '''Pehui-djat''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan}}, which comprised three {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan stars). Since all associated {{ill|Celestial object|de|Himmelsobjekt}}s are very faint, no {{ill|Star|de|Stern}} stands out as particularly prominent. The decan stars can be localized within the regions of the constellations {{ill|Hydra (constellation)|de|Wasserschlange (Sternbild)}} and {{ill|Vela (constellation)|de|Segel des Schiffs}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Pehui-djat represented the fourth decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|lt=Shemu#Dating|lt=6th Schemu II|de|6. Schemu II}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * {{Ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Pehui-djat represented the fourth decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|lt=Shemu#Dating|lt=6th Schemu II|de|6. Schemu II}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 95. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. Since all associated {{ill|Celestial object|de|Himmelsobjekt|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}s are very faint, no {{ill|Star|de|Stern|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} stands out as particularly prominent. The decan stars can be localized within the regions of the constellations {{ill|Hydra (constellation)|de|Wasserschlange (Sternbild)|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Vela (constellation)|de|Segel des Schiffs|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, Pehui-djat represented the fourth decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} was set for the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|lt=Shemu#Dating|lt=6th Schemu II|de|6. Schemu II|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 95. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Decan star (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Pehui-djat]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:ペフイ・ジャット]] [[en:draft:Pehui-djat]] [[de:Pehui-djat]] [[sn:Pehui-djat]] [[sn:ehui-djat]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = F22-G43-Z1*Z1 U28-M17-M17-t N14-N33:N33:N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Pehui-djat
''Pḥ.wj-ḏ3j.t'' |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = Pehui-djat }} '''Pehui-djat''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which comprised three {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (decan stars). Since all associated {{ill|Celestial object|de|Himmelsobjekt|qq=draft|qq=draft}}s are very faint, no {{ill|Star|de|Stern|qq=draft|qq=draft}} stands out as particularly prominent. The decan stars can be localized within the regions of the constellations {{ill|Hydra (constellation)|de|Wasserschlange (Sternbild)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Vela (constellation)|de|Segel des Schiffs|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Pehui-djat represented the fourth decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was set for the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|lt=Shemu#Dating|lt=6th Schemu II|de|6. Schemu II|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 95. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Decan star (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Pehui-djat]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pehui-djat]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]] }} [[ja:ペフイ・ジャット]] [[en:draft:Pehui-djat]] [[de:Pehui-djat]] [[sn:Pehui-djat]] [[sn:ehui-djat]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Pehui-djat]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Ja import failed]] {{translated page|de|Pehui-djat|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}}

{{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = F22-G43-Z1*Z1 U28-M17-M17-t N14-N33:N33:N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Pehui-djat
''Pḥ.wj-ḏ3j.t'' |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = Pehui-djat }} '''Pehui-djat''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egypt|qq=}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Decade (Ancient Egypt)|13=User:Immanuelle/Decan|qq=}}, which comprised three {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=}} (decan stars). Since all associated {{ill|Celestial object|de|Himmelsobjekt|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Celestial object|qq=}}s are very faint, no {{ill|Star|de|Stern|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Star|qq=}} stands out as particularly prominent. The decan stars can be localized within the regions of the constellations {{ill|Hydra (constellation)|de|Wasserschlange (Sternbild)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hydra (constellation)|qq=}} and {{ill|Vela (constellation)|de|Segel des Schiffs|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vela (constellation)|qq=}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qq=}}, Pehui-djat represented the fourth decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heliacal rising|qq=}} was set for the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|lt=Shemu#Dating|lt=6th Schemu II|de|6. Schemu II|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Harvest|qq=}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senusret III|qq=}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|qq=}}) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Christian Leitz|qq=}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. {{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = F22-G43-Z1*Z1 U28-M17-M17-t N14-N33:N33:N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Pehui-djat
''Pḥ.wj-ḏ3j.t'' |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = Pehui-djat }} '''Pehui-djat''' is the designation of an [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]] [[:en:Decan|Decan]], which comprised three {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=}} (decan stars). Since all associated {{ill|Celestial object|de|Himmelsobjekt|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Celestial object|qq=}}s are very faint, no [[:en:Star|Star]] stands out as particularly prominent. The decan stars can be localized within the regions of the constellations [[:en:Hydra (constellation)|Hydra (constellation)]] and [[:en:Vela (constellation)|Vela (constellation)]]. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qq=}}, Pehui-djat represented the fourth decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=}}. The [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] was set for the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|lt=Shemu#Dating|lt=6th Schemu II|de|6. Schemu II|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Harvest|qq=}}, based on the arrangement ordered under [[:en:Senusret III|Senusret III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 95. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The decan stars can be localized within the regions of the constellations [[:en:Hydra (constellation)|Hydra (constellation)]] and [[:en:Vela (constellation)|Vela (constellation)]]. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qq=}}, Pehui-djat represented the fourth decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=}}. The [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] was set for the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|lt=Shemu#Dating|lt=6th Schemu II|de|6. Schemu II|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Harvest|qq=}}, based on the arrangement ordered under [[:en:Senusret III|Senusret III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 95. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. Since all associated {{ill|Celestial object|de|Himmelsobjekt|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Celestial object|qq=}}s are very faint, no [[:en:Star|Star]] stands out as particularly prominent. The decan stars can be localized within the regions of the constellations [[:en:Hydra (constellation)|Hydra (constellation)]] and [[:en:Vela (constellation)|Vela (constellation)]]. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qq=}}, Pehui-djat represented the fourth decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=}}. The [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] was set for the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|lt=Shemu#Dating|lt=6th Schemu II|de|6. Schemu II|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Harvest|qq=}}, based on the arrangement ordered under [[:en:Senusret III|Senusret III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 95. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. {{translated page|de|Pehui-djat|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decan Stars]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[sn:Pehui-djat]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[sn:Pehui-djat]] }} [[sn:Pehui-djat]] [[ja:ペフイ・ジャット]] [[de:Pehui-djat]] [[sn:ehui-djat]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decan Stars]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. {{translated page|de|Pehui-djat|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:ペフイ・ジャット]] [[de:Pehui-djat]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] [[Category:qq]] {{wikidata link|Q2067507}} [[Category:Pages linked to by wikidata]] [[Category:Matching pagename on wikidata]]

[[de:Pehui-djat]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q2067507]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:23, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:24, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = F22-G43-Z1*Z1 U28-M17-M17-t N14-N33:N33:N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Pehui-djat
''Pḥ.wj-ḏ3j.t'' |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = Pehui-djat }} '''Pehui-djat''' is the designation of an [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]] [[:en:Decan|Decan]], which comprised three {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=}} (decan stars). Since all associated {{ill|Celestial object|de|Himmelsobjekt|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Celestial object|qq=}}s are very faint, no [[:en:Star|Star]] stands out as particularly prominent. The decan stars can be localized within the regions of the constellations [[:en:Hydra (constellation)|Hydra (constellation)]] and [[:en:Vela (constellation)|Vela (constellation)]]. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qq=}}, Pehui-djat represented the fourth decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=}}. The [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] was set for the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|lt=Shemu#Dating|lt=6th Schemu II|de|6. Schemu II|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Harvest|qq=}}, based on the arrangement ordered under [[:en:Senusret III|Senusret III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 95. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] was set for the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|lt=Shemu#Dating|lt=6th Schemu II|de|6. Schemu II|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Harvest|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, based on the arrangement ordered under [[:en:Senusret III|Senusret III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. The [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] was set for the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|lt=Shemu#Dating|lt=6th Schemu II|de|6. Schemu II|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Harvest|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, based on the arrangement ordered under [[:en:Senusret III|Senusret III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. ''Pḥ.wj-ḏ3j.t'' |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = Pehui-djat }} '''Pehui-djat''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|en|Ancient Egypt|WD=Q11768|lt=Ancient Egypt}} {{ill|Decan|en|Decan|WD=Q5248738|lt=Decan}}, which comprised three {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=|WD=Q804527}} (decan stars). Since all associated {{ill|Celestial object|de|Himmelsobjekt|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Celestial object|qq=|WD=Q6999}}s are very faint, no {{ill|Star|en|Star|WD=Q523|lt=Star}} stands out as particularly prominent. The decan stars can be localized within the regions of the constellations {{ill|Hydra (constellation)|en|Hydra (constellation)|WD=Q10578|lt=Hydra (constellation)}} and {{ill|Vela (constellation)|en|Vela (constellation)|WD=Q10521|lt=Vela (constellation)}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qq=|WD=Q2005559}}, Pehui-djat represented the fourth decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=|WD=Q165496}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|en|Heliacal rising|WD=Q368373|lt=Heliacal rising}} was set for the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|lt=Shemu#Dating|lt=6th Schemu II|de|6. Schemu II|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Harvest|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|en|Senusret III|WD=Q19248|lt=Senusret III}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|en|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|WD=Q719639|lt=Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt}}) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|WD=Q1080802|lt=Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 95. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|WD=Q2643704}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. {{translated page|de|Pehui-djat|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} [[ja:ペフイ・ジャット]] [[de:Pehui-djat]] {{wikidata link|Q2067507}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]
